When one product has very rapid and the other has rapid dissolution, similarity must be demonstrated.
m9_biowaiver_rules.threshold_values: Very rapid: ≥85% dissolved in ≤15 min; Rapid: ≥85% dissolved in ≤30 min; f2 ≥50 for similarity
m9_biowaiver_rules.source_quote: To qualify for a BCS-based biowaiver for BCS Class I drug substances both the test product and reference product should display either very rapid (≥85% for the mean percent dissolved in ≤ 15 minutes) in vitro dissolution characteristics, or rapid (≥85% for the mean percent dissolved in ≤ 30 minutes) and similar in vitro dissolution characteristics (i.e., based on f2 comparison), under all of the defined conditions.
m9_biowaiver_rules.jurisdiction_note: Harmonised; f2 comparison for rapid dissolution is key addition vs some regional approaches

m9_biowaiver_rules.rule: BCS Class III biowaiver dissolution criteria: Both test and reference products must display VERY RAPID dissolution (≥85% dissolved in ≤15 minutes) under ALL defined conditions. The stricter very-rapid requirement (vs Class I's rapid option) reflects the tighter excipient constraints required for Class III.
m9_biowaiver_rules.threshold_values: Very rapid ONLY: ≥85% dissolved in ≤15 minutes; no f2-based option available for Class III
m9_biowaiver_rules.source_quote: To qualify for a BCS-based biowaiver for BCS Class III drug substances both the test product and reference product should display very rapid (≥85% for the mean percent dissolved in ≤15 minutes) in vitro dissolution characteristics under the defined conditions.
m9_biowaiver_rules.jurisdiction_note: Harmonised; Class III very-rapid requirement is stricter than Class I option; ensures less reliance on dissolution to drive bioequivalence

m9_biowaiver_rules.rule: Dissolution profile similarity (f2 factor) evaluation requires: minimum 3 time points (zero excluded); same time points for both products; mean values used; not more than one mean value ≥85% dissolved for either product; coefficient of variation ≤20% at early time points (up to 10 min); ≤10% at other time points. Two profiles are similar when f2 ≥50.
m9_biowaiver_rules.threshold_values: f2 ≥50 = similar; min 3 time points; CV ≤20% early, ≤10% other; only one time point ≥85%
m9_biowaiver_rules.source_quote: The evaluation of the similarity factor is based on the following conditions: A minimum of three time points (zero excluded). The time points should be the same for the two products. Mean of the individual values for every time point for each product. Not more than one mean value of ≥85% dissolved for either of the products. To allow the use of mean data, the coefficient of variation should not be more than 20% at early time-points (up to 10 minutes), and should not be more than 10% at other time points. Two dissolution profiles are considered similar when the f2 value is ≥50.
m9_biowaiver_rules.jurisdiction_note: Harmonised; f2 method widely used across FDA, EMA, PMDA

m9_biowaiver_rules.rule: When both test and reference products demonstrate ≥85% dissolved in 15 minutes, f2 comparison is unnecessary; profiles are automatically considered similar.
m9_biowaiver_rules.threshold_values: Both products ≥85% at ≤15 min → f2 calculation not required
m9_biowaiver_rules.source_quote: When both test and reference products demonstrate that ≥85% of the labelled amount of the drug is dissolved in 15 minutes, comparison with an f2 test is unnecessary and the dissolution profiles are considered similar.

m9_biowaiver_rules.rule: For products with more than one strength, the BCS biowaiver approach must be applied for EACH strength individually; test and reference product dissolution profiles must be compared at each strength.
m9_biowaiver_rules.threshold_values: Per-strength comparison required; no extrapolation from one strength to another
m9_biowaiver_rules.source_quote: For products with more than one strength, the BCS approach should be applied for each strength, i.e., it is expected that test and reference product dissolution profiles are compared at each strength. No; a BCS-based biowaiver requires supporting data for each strength in a product series.

m9_biowaiver_rules.rule: FDC dissolution criteria: FDCs with only BCS Class I drugs must meet Class I dissolution criteria. FDCs with only BCS Class III drugs or mixed Class I and III must meet Class III dissolution criteria (very rapid, ≥85% in ≤15 min). For FDCs with both Class I and III, the Class-specific criteria apply per component.
m9_biowaiver_rules.threshold_values: Class I FDC: ≥85% in ≤30 min (rapid) with f2; Class III or mixed FDC: ≥85% in ≤15 min (very rapid)
m9_biowaiver_rules.source_quote: FDC formulations containing only BCS Class I drugs should meet dissolution criteria for a BCS Class I drug. FDC formulations containing only BCS Class III drugs should meet dissolution criteria for a BCS Class III drug. For FDCs containing both BCS Class I and BCS Class III drugs the dissolution criteria for the applicable BCS class for each component should be applied.

m9_biowaiver_rules.rule: For Caco-2 passive transport validation, passive transport must be demonstrated: efflux ratio (P_appBL→AP / P_appAP→BL) must be <2 at studied drug concentrations. Method validation requires rank-order relationships with model drugs characterising zero, low (<50%), moderate (50-84%), and high (≥85%) permeability (minimum 5 per group + zero permeability marker).
m9_biowaiver_rules.threshold_values: Efflux ratio <2 for passive transport; high permeability model drugs fa ≥85%; minimum 5 model drugs per permeability group + zero marker; minimum 3 cell assay replicates
m9_biowaiver_rules.source_quote: Passive transport of the test compound should be demonstrated... ratio of apparent permeability (Papp) between the basolateral-to-apical and apical-to-basolateral directions <2 for the selected drug concentrations... The suitability of the Caco-2 cell assays for BCS permeability determination should be demonstrated by establishing a rank-order relationship between experimental permeability values and the extent of drug absorption in human subjects using zero, low (<50%), moderate (50–84%), and high (≥85%) permeability model drugs. A sufficient number of model drugs are recommended for the validation to characterize high, moderate and low permeability (a minimum 5 for each), plus a zero permeability marker.
m9_biowaiver_rules.jurisdiction_note: Caco-2 method primarily standardised via FDA experience; adopted into M9 as harmonised standard

m9_biowaiver_rules.rule: Drug substances with non-linear pharmacokinetics are still eligible for a BCS-based biowaiver if they meet the solubility and permeability criteria for BCS Class I or III classification.
m9_biowaiver_rules.threshold_values: Non-linear PK does not disqualify; eligibility based on solubility and permeability classification
m9_biowaiver_rules.source_quote: Drug substances that exhibit non-linear pharmacokinetics are eligible for a BCS-based biowaiver if they meet the solubility and permeability criteria for BCS Class I or III classification.

m9_biowaiver_rules.rule: When different salt forms of the same drug substance are used in test vs reference product, the dose of the specific active moiety must be identical irrespective of salt forms; weight change associated with different salt forms is NOT relevant to the solubility assessment. A biowaiver may be applicable if both salts belong to BCS Class I (high solubility AND high permeability).
m9_biowaiver_rules.threshold_values: Dose of active moiety must be identical; biowaiver only if both salt forms are BCS Class I
m9_biowaiver_rules.source_quote: The BCS classifies a specific drug substance. The dose of the specific active moiety needs to be identical irrespective of the salt forms. Hence, there is no relevance for weight change. A BCS-based biowaiver may be applicable if the test and reference products contain different (simple) salts, provided that both belong to BCS Class I (high solubility and high permeability).

m9_biowaiver_rules.rule: For dissolution profile comparison between Class I products where one has very rapid (≥85% in ≤15 min) and the other has rapid (≥85% in ≤30 min) dissolution, sufficient sampling points must be taken to calculate f2 to demonstrate similarity.
m9_biowaiver_rules.threshold_values: f2 must be calculated; sufficient sampling points at ≤15 min needed
m9_biowaiver_rules.source_quote: If one product exhibits dissolution at greater than 85% at 15 minutes but the other does not, sufficient sampling points should be taken to calculate f2 to demonstrate similarity.

m9_biowaiver_rules.rule: Uncoated tablets and non-functional film-coated tablets are considered the same dosage form and a comparison between these is eligible for a BCS-based biowaiver. Tablets and capsules are NOT considered the same dosage form; a BCS-based biowaiver comparison between tablets and capsules may not be acceptable in principle.
m9_biowaiver_rules.threshold_values: Same dosage form requirement: uncoated = non-functional film-coated tablets; tablets ≠ capsules
m9_biowaiver_rules.source_quote: Uncoated tablets and non-functional film-coated tablets are considered to be the same dosage form; a comparison between these dosage forms would be eligible for a BCS-based biowaiver. Tablets and capsules are not considered to be the same dosage form and in principle a BCS-based biowaiver may not be acceptable (see also response to 3.1).

class_I.solubility: High — highest single therapeutic dose completely soluble in ≤250 mL aqueous media over pH 1.2-6.8 at 37±1°C
class_I.permeability: High — absolute bioavailability ≥85% OR ≥85% dose recovered in urine as parent + Phase 1/Phase 2 metabolites
class_I.dissolution_requirement: Very rapid (≥85% in ≤15 min) OR Rapid (≥85% in ≤30 min) with f2 ≥50
class_I.excipient_requirement: Absorption-affecting excipients: qualitatively same, within ±10% quantitatively; cumulative ±10%. Other excipients: differences permitted.
class_I.notes: Lowest risk for excipient effects on absorption; BCS Class I biowaivers most broadly accepted

class_II.solubility: Low — does not meet the ≤250 mL / pH 1.2-6.8 high solubility criterion
class_II.dissolution_requirement: N/A — not eligible for BCS biowaiver
class_II.notes: Dissolution rate-limiting; BCS biowaiver not applicable under M9

class_III.solubility: High — highest single therapeutic dose completely soluble in ≤250 mL aqueous media over pH 1.2-6.8 at 37±1°C
class_III.permeability: Low — does not meet ≥85% bioavailability or urine recovery criterion
class_III.dissolution_requirement: Very rapid ONLY — ≥85% in ≤15 minutes under ALL test conditions
class_III.excipient_requirement: All excipients qualitatively same and quantitatively similar (except film coat/capsule shell, colorants, flavoring). Absorption-affecting excipients: within ±10% per excipient, ±10% cumulative. All excipients must meet Table 1 limits relative to core weight.
class_III.notes: Permeability rate-limiting; stricter excipient and dissolution requirements than Class I; site-specific absorption possible

class_IV.dissolution_requirement: N/A — not eligible for BCS biowaiver
class_IV.notes: Both solubility and permeability limiting; BCS biowaiver not applicable under M9

universal_conditions_required: Drug substance must be BCS Class I or III per M9 classification criteria
Drug product must be immediate-release, oral solid dosage form or suspension with systemic action
Drug product must be the same dosage form and strength as the reference product
Drug substances in test and reference must be identical (same active moiety form — no different esters, ethers, isomers, complexes, derivatives)
Narrow therapeutic index drugs are excluded
Buccal and sublingual absorption products are excluded
Administration must include water (products intended for dosing without water require additional BE study without water)
Excipient criteria per Section 3.1 must be satisfied
In vitro dissolution criteria per Section 3.2 must be satisfied under all three pH conditions
Regulatory agreement/approval in the applicable region(s)

class_I_specific_conditions.dissolution: Both test and reference must achieve ≥85% dissolved in ≤30 min (rapid) with demonstrated similarity (f2 ≥50), OR both achieve ≥85% in ≤15 min (very rapid) — f2 unnecessary if both very rapid
class_I_specific_conditions.excipients: Absorption-affecting excipients qualitatively same and within ±10%; cumulative ±10%; other excipients may differ qualitatively/quantitatively

class_III_specific_conditions.dissolution: Both test and reference must achieve ≥85% dissolved in ≤15 min (very rapid) under all defined conditions — no f2-based option
class_III_specific_conditions.excipients: All excipients must be qualitatively the same and quantitatively similar per Table 1 (except film coat, capsule shell, colorants, flavoring); absorption-affecting excipients within ±10% per excipient and cumulative

fdc_conditions.rule: ALL drug substances in FDC must be BCS Class I or III; FDC dissolution criteria determined by most restrictive class present
fdc_conditions.class_I_only_fdc: Meet Class I dissolution and excipient criteria
fdc_conditions.class_III_only_or_mixed_fdc: Must meet Class III dissolution criteria (very rapid ≥85% in ≤15 min) and Class III excipient criteria

exceptions_and_special_cases.salt_forms: Biowaiver may apply if test and reference contain different simple salts provided BOTH are BCS Class I; dose of active moiety must be identical
exceptions_and_special_cases.nonlinear_pk: Non-linear pharmacokinetics does not disqualify from BCS biowaiver if solubility and permeability criteria are met
exceptions_and_special_cases.solubility_borderline: If highest single dose doesn't meet high solubility but highest reference strength is soluble under conditions, may still qualify with demonstration of dose-proportional PK (AUC and Cmax)
exceptions_and_special_cases.different_strengths: Each strength requires separate dissolution comparison; no extrapolation between strengths
exceptions_and_special_cases.tablet_vs_capsule: Tablets and capsules not considered same dosage form; BCS biowaiver not applicable in principle for tablet-to-capsule comparisons
exceptions_and_special_cases.uncoated_vs_film_coated: Uncoated and non-functional film-coated tablets considered same dosage form; comparison eligible for biowaiver
